project_title: Applied History - lab for using historical case study to inform contemporary policy
product_name: Applied History - lab for using historical case study to inform contemporary policy
runtime_target: local-first web application or desktop module with persistent project state
operational_end_state: A researcher can enter a project, use named instruments, observe state changes, and export a governed work product.
minimum_operational_release: A runnable slice of Applied History - lab for using historical case study to inform contemporary policy: one complete workflow from input to output, with fixture, state record, export, acceptance test, and source-bound claim boundary.
full_build_definition: A runnable local or web application with a defined user flow, input fixture, persisted or exportable output, source-bound mechanism, acceptance tests, and a reviewer-visible claim ceiling.
source_actions_before_claim: Complete 4 close-read contract(s), update 12 linked proof-capsule field(s), and preserve the current claim ceiling until passage notes exist.
claim_boundary: This is a full-build plan, not a claim that the full operational system has already been completed.
public_claim_now: May claim a full-build candidate exists with proof and source contracts; do not claim deployment, outside validation, or production readiness until live execution and passage verification are recorded.
current_status: source-backed proof build; needs source-to-output capture and close-read strengthening
blocking_gates: passage-level close-read notes | captured runnable workflow
first_full_build: Add a replayable demo with before/after state, failure mode, and exported result so the software utility is visible rather than asserted.
launch_or_review_path: Create or open the local runtime slice for `applied-history`, then execute the fixture-to-output workflow and capture the result.

instruments.purpose: Turn the `state model` module into a concrete input-operation-output step for Applied History - lab for using historical case study to inform contemporary policy.
instruments.input_contract: Project fixture, source duty, current claim boundary, and required output state.
instruments.operation: Execute or inspect the state model step against the project fixture and record the state change it is supposed to produce.
instruments.output_contract: A reviewer-visible state record, output artifact, or decision boundary tied to the project acceptance tests.
instruments.acceptance_condition: The state model instrument is present, named, connected to the fixture, and has an output or decision field that can be reviewed.

acceptance_tests: claim ceiling test: public claims cannot exceed built evidence
source test: metadata-routed sources require close reading before citation or quotation
privacy test: no local paths or private directories in public deploy
state-transition test: visible input changes recorded state and output

fixture.fixture_name: Applied History - lab for using historical case study to inform contemporary policy first operational slice
fixture.input: Add a replayable demo with before/after state, failure mode, and exported result so the software utility is visible rather than asserted.
fixture.operation: Run the room engine through each required instrument, then produce a reviewer-visible state record and claim boundary.
fixture.expected_output: A runnable slice of Applied History - lab for using historical case study to inform contemporary policy: one complete workflow from input to output, with fixture, state record, export, acceptance test, and source-bound claim boundary.

export_schema.instrument_results: one result object per required module
export_schema.acceptance_results: one result object per required test
export_schema.claim_boundary: public claim ceiling after this run
export_schema.full_underlying_system_complete: always false unless backed by actual external runtime evidence
